summary: The implementation covers most requirements with Internet Identity authentication, user profiles, matching algorithm, swap requests, messaging system, and admin panel. However, several acceptance criteria are missing or not verifiable from the diff, particularly around messaging restrictions, duplicate request prevention, and admin approval functionality.

missing_requirements.requirement: Users can only message matched users
missing_requirements.reason: The backend message sending function (sendMessage) does not check if users have an accepted swap request before allowing messaging. The hasAcceptedSwapRequest helper exists but is not called in sendMessage.

missing_requirements.requirement: Prevent duplicate requests between same users
missing_requirements.reason: The sendSwapRequest function in backend does not implement validation to prevent duplicate requests. While the frontend button checks for existing requests, backend validation is missing.

The backend tracks user approval/blocked status is not implemented. Only verification and blocking are present. No approval workflow or status tracking exists in the UserProfile type or backend functions.

missing_requirements.requirement: Approve users action in admin dashboard
missing_requirements.reason: The admin dashboard UI only displays verify and block actions. No approve action button or approval status is shown in the user table.

hallucinated_features.description: Dark mode theme support via ThemeProvider
hallucinated_features.reason: The implementation includes dark mode styling and a ThemeProvider component, which was not requested in any requirement or acceptance criteria.

The findMatches function restricts matching to only verified users. The requirement specifies matching logic based on cities, company, and role but does not mention verification as a matching prerequisite.
The backend includes _initializeAccessControlWithSecret function and the frontend calls it with a caffeineAdminToken parameter. This authentication mechanism was not specified in the requirements.
